title: cypripedium calceolus var. planipetalum
intro: Cypripedium calceolus var. planipetalum, also known as the yellow lady's slipper, is a type of orchid native to Northern Europe and Asia, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The yellow lady's slipper is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract for use in herbal remedies, although its use and efficacy are not supported by modern scientific evidence.
